body.title: DickSimnel: detect and block fake DONE posts (planning text != completion)
body.description:
DickSimnel has posted DICKSIMNEL_DONE twice with planning/analysis text as the summary (e.g. 'I'll implement the versioned inference models registry...'). This happens when ToolLoop returns the OR model's first message rather than actual completion output. Root cause: _post_result() accepts any non-None result_text as completion.

Fix: validate result_text before calling _post_result. A result is not a completion unless it contains evidence of actual work: a DONE indicator, commit hash pattern, test results, or explicit 'Completion:' section. When the validation fails, call _escalate_ticket() instead so CC can fix what DickSimnel left undone.

**Affected files:** devices/dicksimnel/device.py, devices/dicksimnel/toolloop.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; OOP-first; log validation failures at WARNING
**Scope boundary:** In scope: validate result before posting DONE; escalate on planning-text detection. Out of scope: fixing ToolLoop iteration limit, redesigning the inference loop.
**Completion criteria:** (1) _post_result validates result_text with a heuristic (no DONE keyword + short + starts with plan phrase → escalate); (2) test: planning text → escalate; genuine completion → done; (3) DickSimnel.DONE channel posts contain actual results not planning text
